reasoning: The Lean theorem matches the informal statement exactly. `Entire f` is defined as `AnalyticOn ℂ f Set.univ`, which corresponds to the standard definition of an entire function (analytic on all of ℂ, verified via Mathlib's `Analysis/Analytic/Basic.lean`). The hypothesis `∀ z, 1 ≤ abs (f z)` (with `open Complex` making `abs` resolve to `Complex.abs`) is precisely |f(z)| ≥ 1 for all z ∈ ℂ. The conclusion `∃ c : ℂ, ∀ z, f z = c` is the standard Lean encoding of "f is constant". No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the types match. The auxiliary axioms (`bounded_reciprocal_function`, `liouville_theorem`, `constant_reciprocal_function`) are only used as proof scaffolding and do not alter the statement of `constant_entire_function`.
